scope.summary: R1 adds a binary-level external MCP client proof that drives the built oraclemcp artifact over raw stdio and raw Streamable HTTP, without importing server-side MCP helpers.
out_of_scope: scripts/rig harness changes owned by pane 4
authenticated HTTP principal matrix beyond the local --allow-no-auth reachability lane

entry_points.name: pre-fix external-client proof target
entry_points.trace: `git show b16c282b^:crates/oraclemcp/tests/r1_external_client.rs >/dev/null 2>&1; echo pre_fix_test_path_status=$?` printed `pre_fix_test_path_status=128`, proving the previous committed tree had no R1 external-client binary proof target.

entry_points.name: external raw stdio client against installed artifact
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp --test r1_external_client -- --nocapture` exited 0; `installed_artifact_accepts_raw_external_stdio_client` passed after spawning `env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_oraclemcp")`, sending literal newline-delimited JSON-RPC initialize, tools/list, and oracle_query frames, and receiving structured JSON-RPC replies.

entry_points.name: external raw HTTP client against installed artifact
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp --test r1_external_client -- --nocapture` exited 0; `installed_artifact_accepts_raw_external_http_client` passed after spawning `env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_oraclemcp")`, opening `/mcp` over loopback Streamable HTTP, and completing initialize, tools/list, and oracle_query through raw HTTP requests.

entry_points.name: format and whitespace checks
entry_points.trace: `env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo fmt --all -- --check` exited 0, and `git diff --check -- crates/oraclemcp/tests/r1_external_client.rs` exited 0.

limitations: This is an offline local reachability proof over the built Cargo binary artifact; it does not contact a live Oracle database.
HTTP proof uses explicit loopback --allow-no-auth development mode to isolate transport reachability from OAuth/client-credential policy.
